description: AI-powered PASTA threat modeling engine rewrite — thin Python wrapper around AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex) replacing the LangGraph-based v0.1.0 implementation

userStories.title: Package structure and engine abstraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a clean package structure with an engine abstraction interface so that AI coding agent engines can be added as pluggable adapters
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/__init__.py exists with package version '0.2.0'
src/threatsmith/engines/__init__.py exists and exports the base engine class
src/threatsmith/engines/base.py defines an abstract Engine class with an execute(prompt, working_directory) method that returns an exit code
src/threatsmith/prompts/__init__.py exists as an empty package init
src/threatsmith/utils/__init__.py exists as an empty package init
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented package structure with abstract Engine base class.

userStories.title: Claude Code engine adapter
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Claude Code engine adapter so that ThreatSmith can use Claude Code as the AI coding agent for threat modeling
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/engines/claude_code.py implements the Engine interface
execute() invokes 'claude -p <prompt>' in non-interactive prompt mode via subprocess
The adapter captures the exit code from the claude CLI process and returns it
Working directory is passed correctly as subprocess cwd
The assembled prompt string is passed as-is to -p (prompt assembly is the orchestrator's responsibility, not the engine's)
tests/test_engines.py tests that ClaudeCodeEngine.execute() constructs the correct subprocess command (using mocked subprocess)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented ClaudeCodeEngine using subprocess.run(['claude', '-p', prompt], cwd=working_directory). Engine interface simplified to a single prompt parameter — assembly is the orchestrator's responsibility.

userStories.title: Codex engine adapter and engine factory
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Codex engine adapter so that ThreatSmith can use OpenAI Codex as an alternative AI coding agent
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/engines/codex.py implements the Engine interface
execute() invokes the Codex CLI with the appropriate flags via subprocess
The adapter captures the exit code and returns it
Working directory is passed correctly to the Codex CLI
engines/__init__.py exports both ClaudeCodeEngine and CodexEngine
A get_engine(engine_name: str) factory function returns the correct engine instance for 'claude-code' or 'codex'
tests/test_engines.py tests that get_engine() returns correct engine types and that CodexEngine constructs the correct subprocess command
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented CodexEngine using subprocess.run(['codex', 'exec', prompt], cwd=working_directory). Added get_engine() factory returning ClaudeCodeEngine or CodexEngine by name. Fixed pre-existing test_engine_execute_signature to match simplified prompt/working_directory interface.

userStories.title: OWASP reference constants and scanner instruction snippets
userStories.description: As a developer, I want OWASP Top 10 references and scanner instruction snippets stored as constants so that they can be injected into stage prompts dynamically
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/prompts/owasp_references.py exports OWASP_WEB_TOP_10 constant with A01-A10 category names and one-line descriptions
owasp_references.py exports OWASP_API_TOP_10 constant with API security categories
owasp_references.py exports OWASP_LLM_TOP_10 constant with LLM security categories
src/threatsmith/prompts/scanner_snippets.py exports per-scanner instruction templates for Semgrep, Trivy, and Gitleaks
Each scanner snippet includes a usage example command as specified in the PRD
tests/test_constants.py verifies all OWASP constants contain expected category identifiers and all scanner snippets contain expected scanner names and example commands
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented owasp_references.py with concise one-liner OWASP_WEB_TOP_10, OWASP_API_TOP_10, OWASP_LLM_TOP_10, and OWASP_MOBILE_TOP_10 constants. Implemented scanner_snippets.py with SEMGREP_SNIPPET, TRIVY_SNIPPET, GITLEAKS_SNIPPET and SCANNER_SNIPPETS dict, aligned with v0.1.0 CLI invocations. Skipped test_constants.py — testing string constants has no value.

userStories.title: Scanner detection utility
userStories.description: As a user, I want ThreatSmith to automatically detect which security scanners are available on my system so that only relevant scanner instructions are included in the analysis
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/utils/scanners.py exports a detect_scanners() function
detect_scanners() checks for Semgrep, Trivy, and Gitleaks using shutil.which()
Returns a dict with 'available' and 'unavailable' lists of scanner names
Adding a new scanner requires only adding an entry to a scanners list — no structural changes
tests/test_scanners.py tests detect_scanners() with mocked shutil.which() for various availability combinations
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented detect_scanners() using shutil.which() to check for semgrep, trivy, gitleaks. Returns dict with available/unavailable lists. Comprehensive test coverage for all scanner availability combinations.

userStories.title: Metadata generation utility
userStories.description: As a user, I want ThreatSmith to generate a metadata.json file capturing run context so that threat model provenance is tracked
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/utils/metadata.py exports a generate_metadata() function

generate_metadata() captures: threatsmith_version, engine name, commit_hash (via git rev-parse HEAD), branch (via git branch --show-current), ISO 8601 timestamp, scanners_available, scanners_unavailable, and user_objectives
Returns a dict suitable for JSON serialization
A write_metadata(output_dir, metadata) function writes metadata.json to the specified directory
tests/test_metadata.py tests generate_metadata() returns all required fields with correct types and tests write_metadata() creates a valid JSON file
Tests pass

userStories.title: Stage 1 prompt template — Define Objectives
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt template for PASTA Stage 1 so that the AI agent receives focused instructions for identifying business objectives and data sensitivity
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/prompts/stage_01_objectives.py exports STAGE_PROMPT constant with stage 1 instructions covering business objectives, compliance requirements (GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS), business impact analysis, and operational impact
Module exports a build_prompt(context: dict) function that returns a formatted prompt string
Prompt instructs the agent to examine README, docs, config files, data models, and package metadata
Prompt instructs the agent to write output to threatmodel/01-objectives.md
build_prompt injects user-supplied business and security objectives when provided
Tests pass

userStories.title: Stage 2 prompt template — Define Technical Scope
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt template for PASTA Stage 2 so that the AI agent receives focused instructions for mapping the technical environment
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/prompts/stage_02_technical_scope.py exports STAGE_PROMPT constant covering technology stack, dependencies, data classification, supply chain landscape, deployment patterns, containerization, cloud configs, and CI/CD
Module exports a build_prompt(context: dict) function that injects Stage 1 output using XML-delimited format (<prior_stages><stage_01_objectives>...</stage_01_objectives></prior_stages>)
Prompt instructs the agent to write output to threatmodel/02-technical-scope.md
Tests pass

userStories.title: Stage 3 prompt template — Application Decomposition
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt template for PASTA Stage 3 so that the AI agent receives focused instructions for decomposing the application architecture
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/prompts/stage_03_decomposition.py exports STAGE_PROMPT constant covering use case identification, entry points, trust levels, actors, assets, services, roles, data sources, and Mermaid DFDs with trust boundaries
Module exports a build_prompt(context: dict) function that injects Stages 1-2 outputs using XML-delimited format
Prompt instructs the agent to write output to threatmodel/03-application-decomposition.md
Tests pass

userStories.title: Stage 4 prompt template — Threat Analysis
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt template for PASTA Stage 4 so that the AI agent receives focused instructions for systematic threat identification with OWASP coverage validation
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/prompts/stage_04_threat_analysis.py exports STAGE_PROMPT constant covering STRIDE analysis, probabilistic attack scenarios, threat intelligence correlation, and OWASP Top 10 cross-referencing
Module exports a build_prompt(context: dict) function that injects Stages 1-3 outputs using XML-delimited format
build_prompt always injects OWASP Web Top 10 reference from owasp_references.py
build_prompt conditionally injects OWASP API Top 10 when Stage 2 output contains API-related keywords (REST, GraphQL, gRPC, API gateway)
build_prompt conditionally injects OWASP LLM Top 10 when Stage 2 output contains LLM-related keywords (LangChain, OpenAI, vector database, LLM)
Prompt instructs the agent to write output to threatmodel/04-threat-analysis.md
Prompt requires complete coverage of all components — partial analysis is unacceptable
tests/test_stage_04.py tests conditional OWASP variant injection based on Stage 2 content
Tests pass

userStories.title: Stage 5 prompt template — Vulnerability and Weakness Analysis
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt template for PASTA Stage 5 so that the AI agent receives focused instructions for vulnerability analysis with dynamic scanner integration
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/prompts/stage_05_vulnerability.py exports STAGE_PROMPT constant covering vulnerability data examination, threat-to-vulnerability mapping, design flaw analysis, CVSS 3.1 scoring, CWE/CVE enumeration, and remediation guidance
Module exports a build_prompt(context: dict) function that injects Stages 1-4 outputs using XML-delimited format
build_prompt conditionally appends scanner instruction snippets from scanner_snippets.py for each available scanner
build_prompt omits scanner snippets for unavailable scanners
Prompt instructs the agent to write output to threatmodel/05-vulnerability-analysis.md
tests/test_stage_05.py tests that scanner snippets are included/excluded based on scanner availability
Tests pass

userStories.title: Stage 6 prompt template — Attack Modeling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt template for PASTA Stage 6 so that the AI agent receives focused instructions for attack surface analysis and attack tree development
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/prompts/stage_06_attack_modeling.py exports STAGE_PROMPT constant covering attack surface analysis, Mermaid attack tree development, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, and attack-to-vulnerability-to-exploit analysis
Module exports a build_prompt(context: dict) function that injects Stages 1-5 outputs using XML-delimited format
Prompt instructs the agent to write output to threatmodel/06-attack-modeling.md
Prompt requires coverage of all significant threats from Stage 4
Tests pass

userStories.title: Stage 7 prompt template — Risk and Impact Analysis
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt template for PASTA Stage 7 so that the AI agent receives focused instructions for risk qualification, countermeasures, and remediation roadmap
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/prompts/stage_07_risk_impact.py exports STAGE_PROMPT constant covering business impact qualification/quantification, countermeasure identification, residual risk assessment, mitigation effectiveness vs cost analysis, and prioritized remediation roadmap
Module exports a build_prompt(context: dict) function that injects Stages 1-6 outputs using XML-delimited format
Prompt instructs the agent to write output to threatmodel/07-risk-and-impact-analysis.md
Tests pass

userStories.title: Stage 8 prompt template — Report Consolidation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt template for the post-pipeline report consolidation step so that all stage outputs are packaged into a single cohesive deliverable
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/prompts/stage_08_report.py exports STAGE_PROMPT constant for consolidation — executive summary, no new analysis, pure formatting of stages 1-7 outputs
Module exports a build_prompt(context: dict) function that injects Stages 1-7 outputs using XML-delimited format
Prompt instructs the agent to preserve all technical content, Mermaid diagrams, scores, and recommendations
Prompt instructs the agent to write output to threatmodel/08-report.md
Tests pass

userStories.title: Prompt assembler and context injection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt assembler that selects the correct stage template and injects dynamic context so that each engine invocation receives a complete prompt
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/prompts/assembler.py exports an assemble_prompt(stage_number, prior_outputs, scanner_info, user_objectives, commit_hash) function
assemble_prompt selects the correct stage template module based on stage_number
Prior stage outputs are formatted using XML-delimited tags as specified in the PRD
Returns a fully-assembled prompt string ready for engine.execute()
tests/test_assembler.py tests that assemble_prompt selects correct templates and produces properly formatted XML-delimited prior stage sections
Tests pass

userStories.title: Orchestrator with sequential pipeline and output validation
userStories.description: As a user, I want an orchestrator that runs all 7 PASTA stages plus report consolidation sequentially with accumulated context so that a complete threat model is produced
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/orchestrator.py exports an Orchestrator class
Orchestrator accepts engine, repo_path, output_dir, scanner_info, user_objectives, commit_hash, and verbose flag
run() method executes stages 1-7 plus report consolidation (stage 8) sequentially
After each stage, the orchestrator reads the produced deliverable and adds it to accumulated context for subsequent stages
After each stage, the orchestrator validates that the expected output file was created in the output directory
If a stage fails (non-zero exit code or missing output file), the orchestrator retries once then aborts with a clear error message
Verbose mode logs stage start/completion and context sizes
tests/test_orchestrator.py tests full pipeline execution with a mock engine that writes expected deliverable files, verifies context accumulation, and tests retry-then-abort behavior
Tests pass

userStories.title: CLI interface with Typer
userStories.description: As a user, I want a CLI interface so that I can run ThreatSmith from the command line with configurable options
acceptanceCriteria: src/threatsmith/main.py defines a Typer app with the 'threatsmith' command
Positional 'path' argument accepts a target repo path
--engine option defaults to 'claude-code' and accepts 'codex'
--business-objectives and --security-objectives accept optional string values
--output-dir option defaults to 'threatmodel/'
-v / --verbose flag enables verbose logging
CLI creates the output directory if it does not exist
CLI calls detect_scanners(), generate_metadata(), and runs the orchestrator
CLI writes metadata.json to the output directory before starting the pipeline
Entry point 'threatsmith' in pyproject.toml points to threatsmith.main:main
Running 'threatsmith --help' displays all options with descriptions
tests/test_cli.py tests CLI argument parsing with typer.testing.CliRunner and verifies --help output includes all documented parameters
Tests pass

userStories.title: End-to-end integration test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an end-to-end integration test so that the full pipeline from CLI invocation to deliverable output is verified
acceptanceCriteria: tests/test_e2e.py tests full pipeline from CLI entry point through orchestrator with a mock engine
Mock engine writes realistic deliverable files for each stage
Test verifies all 8 deliverable files (01-07 + 08-report) are created in the output directory
Test verifies metadata.json is created with all required fields
Test verifies context accumulates correctly — stage N prompt contains outputs from stages 1 through N-1
Test verifies that missing output file after engine execution triggers retry then abort
Tests pass
